findings.evidence: grep shows the only callsite is QuadraticSplitting.lean:157; roadmap Targets.lean:74-81 target is the compositum case which uses the per-quadratic-field result, not this conductor bound.
findings.fix: Make it `private theorem`, matching the convention already used in TauCeti/NumberTheory (e.g. SquareClass.lean:133).
findings.issue: not_dvd_exponent_of_minpoly_quadratic is public but is an implementation helper, used only at line 157 inside this file. It is single-generator/whole-field specific (conductor of θ generating K), so the multiquadratic compositum target cannot reuse it; the docstring's Main results lists only the main theorem.

findings.evidence: grep shows the only callsite is QuadraticSplitting.lean:170; docstring Main results lists only quadratic_ncard_primesOver_iff.
findings.fix: Make it `private theorem` (or, if intended for downstream reuse, list it in the file's Main results and justify exposure).
findings.issue: card_monicFactorsMod_quadratic is public but is an implementation helper, used only at line 170. Not part of the stated deliverable surface.

findings.evidence: Line 155 statement: `Nat.card (primesOver (span {(p : ℤ)}) (𝓞 K)) = finrank ℚ K`; roadmap target Targets.lean:78 likewise uses `Nat.card`.
findings.fix: Rename to use `nat_card` (e.g. quadratic_nat_card_primesOver_iff) to match the operation in the statement.
findings.issue: The public name uses `ncard` but the statement uses `Nat.card`, not `Set.ncard`; this misleads discovery (a user grepping the actual operation/Mathlib convention `nat_card` won't match, and `ncard` signals the wrong function).

summary: The deliverable is the single theorem quadratic_ncard_primesOver_iff, but two implementation helpers are exposed as public theorems despite being used only as internal steps. Mark them private (the file already uses `private` elsewhere in this namespace tree).
